Selection Engine — marquee multi-select

Headless selection: the engine owns the selection set + the drag-box hit-testing and emits state. This page renders every tile, the selected highlight, and the marquee box.

Selection (which keys are picked) — NOT reorder (sortable-engine) nor file intake (drag-n-drop-engine). The marquee drags on EMPTY space; pressing a tile is a click. The engine hit-tests SUPPLIED geometry (never the live DOM) and renders nothing.

1 · Photo gallery — click · shift-range · ctrl/⌘-toggle · marquee

Click a tile to select it; Shift-click for a contiguous range (re-extends from the same anchor); Ctrl/⌘-click to toggle a scattered set. Drag on empty space to rubber-band — the box lights tiles up live as it touches them and de-selects as you drag back out. Hold Shift while starting a drag to ADD to the selection. Ctrl/⌘+A select all · Esc clear. One tile is disabled.

0 selected

Drag a box around some photos.

2 · Large virtualized grid — 10,000 items, smooth marquee, off-screen selection

The grid is virtualized (only the visible tiles are in the DOM) using virtualization-engine.js, which computes positions for all 10,000 items. Those positions are fed to the selection engine as DATA, so the marquee hit-tests cached geometry — no getBoundingClientRect per move — and stays smooth. Drag to the top/bottom edge and it auto-scrolls, selecting items that were never even rendered. The selection survives scrolling past them.

0 selected

Tiles are colored by index. The white highlight + check overlay is the consumer painting the emitted selected set.

Live state — what the engine emits

The engine emits { selected:Set, count, anchor, delta:{entered,left}, marquee:{active,rect} } on every change. The consumer renders from it. Latest first:

No selection yet.
